segments.speaker: HOLLY
segments.en: Hello Dr Green – I'm here to talk to you about my work placement.
segments.zh: 格林博士你好——我来是想跟您谈谈我的工作实习。

segments.speaker: TUTOR
segments.en: Oh yes, it's Holly, isn't it?
segments.zh: 哦，好的，你是霍莉吧？

segments.speaker: HOLLY
segments.en: Yes.
segments.zh: 是的。

segments.speaker: TUTOR
segments.en: So, which work placement have you chosen?
segments.zh: 那么，你选了哪个工作实习？

segments.speaker: HOLLY
segments.en: I decided to go for the Orion Stadium placement.
segments.zh: 我决定选猎户座体育场的实习。我要管理的活动是帮助为小学生组织一场体育比赛。

segments.speaker: HOLLY
segments.en: The event I'll be managing is one where I'm helping to set up a sports competition for primary school children.

segments.speaker: TUTOR
segments.en: Yup.
segments.zh: 嗯。那个实习一直很受欢迎——尽管和孩子们打交道可能比你想象的要难。

segments.speaker: TUTOR
segments.en: That's always a popular placement – even though it can be tougher than you think working with children.

segments.speaker: HOLLY
segments.en: I know, but it's the fresh air that attracts me – organising something indoors doesn't have the same appeal, even though it might be fun.
segments.zh: 我知道，但吸引我的是新鲜空气——在室内组织活动没有同样的吸引力，尽管可能很有趣。

segments.speaker: TUTOR
segments.en: OK, so obviously safety's going to be one of your key concerns for this event.
segments.zh: 好的，那么显然安全将是你这次活动的主要关注点之一。

segments.speaker: HOLLY
segments.en: Yes, I've already thought about that.
segments.zh: 是的，我已经考虑过了。我得确保所有设备都没有损坏。

segments.speaker: HOLLY
segments.en: I'll need to make sure none of the equipment's damaged.

segments.speaker: TUTOR
segments.en: Ah well, you'll be working with schools, so the equipment will be their responsibility.
segments.zh: 嗯，你会和学校合作，所以设备由他们负责。不过，场地以及场地上发生的事情由你负责。

segments.speaker: TUTOR
segments.en: However, the grounds and what goes on there will be yours.

segments.speaker: HOLLY
segments.en: Oh I see – that'll include keeping everyone within the boundary once they're in their kit and on the field?
segments.zh: 哦，我明白了——那包括确保每个人穿上装备、进入场地后都待在边界内吗？

segments.speaker: TUTOR
segments.en: Exactly – you'll need to inspect areas like changing rooms as well for anything someone can trip over, but your main priority will be not to lose anyone!
segments.zh: 没错——你还需要检查更衣室等区域，看看有没有什么东西会绊倒人，但你的首要任务是别把人弄丢了！

segments.speaker: HOLLY
segments.en: Right.
segments.zh: 好的。我需要工作人员来帮忙。

segments.speaker: HOLLY
segments.en: I'll need staff to help with that.

segments.speaker: TUTOR
segments.en: And don't forget about the spectators.
segments.zh: 别忘了观众。

segments.speaker: HOLLY
segments.en: Mmm.
segments.zh: 嗯。我在想他们很多都是家长，可以帮忙组织活动。

segments.speaker: HOLLY
segments.en: I was thinking that many of them will be parents, who could help run the event.

segments.speaker: TUTOR
segments.en: I wouldn't rely on that.
segments.zh: 我可不会指望这个。他们更愿意拍自己的孩子，而不是做志愿者。

segments.speaker: TUTOR
segments.en: They'll be more interested in filming their children than volunteering.

segments.speaker: HOLLY
segments.en: I'll need to make sure they don't interfere with events doing that!
segments.zh: 我得确保他们不会因此干扰到活动！

segments.speaker: TUTOR
segments.en: And that's not always easy, especially when a proud parent's trying to get a snap of their child and you want them to move elsewhere.
segments.zh: 这并不总是容易的，尤其是当一位自豪的家长想给孩子拍张照，而你希望他们挪到别处去的时候。

segments.speaker: HOLLY
segments.en: OK.
segments.zh: 好的。那时间安排呢？

segments.speaker: HOLLY
segments.en: What about the scheduling?

segments.speaker: TUTOR
segments.en: With sporting events there are all sorts of things that can alter the timetable – like rain, for instance – though so far, we've always been lucky with that.
segments.zh: 体育赛事中，有很多因素会改变时间表——比如下雨——不过到目前为止，我们在这方面一直很幸运。

segments.speaker: HOLLY
segments.en: Yeah, and I was thinking about what to do if someone got hurt as well.
segments.zh: 是啊，我也在想如果有人受伤了该怎么办。我知道去年那件事造成了严重的延误。

segments.speaker: HOLLY
segments.en: I know that last year that caused a terrible delay.

segments.speaker: TUTOR
segments.en: You have to be prepared for such things.
segments.zh: 你必须为这类事情做好准备。

segments.speaker: HOLLY
segments.en: Oh.
segments.zh: 哦。如果比赛打平了怎么办——你会让队伍继续比直到有人赢吗？

segments.speaker: HOLLY
segments.en: What if a match ends in a draw – do you let the teams keep going until someone wins?

segments.speaker: TUTOR
segments.en: That'll be up to you – and again, you need to plan for it.
segments.zh: 这由你决定——同样，你需要为此做好计划。

segments.speaker: HOLLY
segments.en: Right.
segments.zh: 好的。

segments.speaker: TUTOR
segments.en: Now, the aim of your work placement is to give you the opportunity to develop the skills that an events manager needs.
segments.zh: 现在，你实习的目的是让你有机会培养活动经理所需的技能。所以我们来聊聊这个。

segments.speaker: TUTOR
segments.en: So, let's talk about that a bit.

segments.speaker: HOLLY
segments.en: Well, I think my communication skills are pretty good.
segments.zh: 嗯，我觉得我的沟通能力还不错。我可以打电话跟人沟通，预订场地之类的。

segments.speaker: HOLLY
segments.en: I can talk on the phone to people and book venues and that kind of thing.

segments.speaker: TUTOR
segments.en: Good – just remember it isn't only about what you say.
segments.zh: 很好——但要记住，这不只是你说什么的问题。比如，如果你面对面见到某人，想说服他们成为赞助商……

segments.speaker: TUTOR
segments.en: If you meet someone face-to-face and want to persuade them to be a sponsor, for example ...

segments.speaker: HOLLY
segments.en: Oh, I'll dress up for that!
segments.zh: 哦，那我肯定会穿得正式一点！没问题。

segments.speaker: HOLLY
segments.en: Sure.

segments.speaker: TUTOR
segments.en: Good.
segments.zh: 好。我们接着聊聊你的组织能力。你从事的是一个非常以人为本的行业，这意味着事情不会总按计划进行。我觉得关键是要准备好做出改变。

segments.speaker: TUTOR
segments.en: Let's go on to think about your organisational skills.

segments.speaker: TUTOR
segments.en: You're working in a very people-based industry and that means things won't always go to plan.

segments.speaker: TUTOR
segments.en: I guess it's being prepared to make changes that matters.

segments.speaker: HOLLY
segments.en: That's right.
segments.zh: 没错。可能因为一个你没想到的问题，你不得不临时调整时间表……

segments.speaker: HOLLY
segments.en: You may have to make an on-the-spot change to a timetable because of a problem you hadn't anticipated ...

segments.speaker: TUTOR
segments.en: ... just do it!
segments.zh: ……那就去做！好的。

segments.speaker: TUTOR
segments.en: OK.

segments.speaker: HOLLY
segments.en: How's your time management these days?
segments.zh: 你最近时间管理得怎么样？

segments.speaker: TUTOR
segments.en: I'm working on it – I'm certainly better when I have a deadline, which is why this work suits me.
segments.zh: 我正在努力改进——有截止日期的时候我肯定做得更好，所以这份工作很适合我。

segments.speaker: HOLLY
segments.en: Yes, but it's how you respond as that deadline approaches!
segments.zh: 是的，但关键是你怎么应对那个截止日期临近的时候！

segments.speaker: TUTOR
segments.en: I know I've got to look calm even if I'm in a panic.
segments.zh: 我知道即使心里很慌，表面上也得装得冷静。

segments.speaker: HOLLY
segments.en: Just think to yourself – no one must know I'm under pressure.
segments.zh: 你就想——绝对不能让任何人知道我压力很大。

segments.speaker: HOLLY
segments.en: Yeah – even though I'm multi-tasking like crazy!
segments.zh: 是啊——哪怕我同时忙得不可开交！

segments.speaker: TUTOR
segments.en: Another skill that events managers need is creativity.
segments.zh: 活动经理需要的另一个技能是创造力。通常客户有一个我们称之为“大蓝图”的想法，但需要活动经理来思考所有让这个想法实现的细节。

segments.speaker: TUTOR
segments.en: Often your client has what we call the 'big picture' idea, but it's up to the events manager to think of all the fine points that go to making it work.

segments.speaker: HOLLY
segments.en: Right, so I need to listen carefully to that idea and then fill in all the gaps.
segments.zh: 对，所以我需要仔细听那个想法，然后填补所有的空白。

segments.speaker: TUTOR
segments.en: That's right.
segments.zh: 没错。而且你手下会有一个团队，所以另一个关键技能是领导力。你的团队可能也有很多想法，但最终决策得由你来做。比如，茶点是在室内还是室外供应？

segments.speaker: TUTOR
segments.en: And you'll have a team working under you, so another key skill is leadership.

segments.speaker: TUTOR
segments.en: Your team may have lots of ideas too, but you've got to make the ultimate choices.

segments.speaker: TUTOR
segments.en: Do we have refreshments inside or out, for example?

segments.speaker: HOLLY
segments.en: Isn't it better to be democratic?
segments.zh: 民主一点不是更好吗？

segments.speaker: TUTOR
segments.en: It's a nice idea, but you have the ultimate responsibility.
segments.zh: 想法是好的，但最终责任在你。所以，要相信你认为最好的。准备好说“是的，这是个好主意，但在这里行不通”。

segments.speaker: TUTOR
segments.en: So, believe in what you think best.

segments.speaker: TUTOR
segments.en: Be prepared to say 'yes', that's a good idea but it won't work here.

segments.speaker: HOLLY
segments.en: I see what you mean.
segments.zh: 我明白你的意思。那社交方面呢？我知道这是很多学生担心的地方，因为我们没什么经验可以提供给他人。

segments.speaker: HOLLY
segments.en: What about the networking side of things?

segments.speaker: HOLLY
segments.en: I know it's an area that a lot of students worry about because we don't have much experience to offer others.

segments.speaker: TUTOR
segments.en: But even without it – you can still be an interesting person with useful ideas.
segments.zh: 但即使没有经验——你仍然可以是一个有趣的人，有有用的想法。而且你给人留下的印象越深越好。

segments.speaker: TUTOR
segments.en: And the more people you impress, the better.

segments.speaker: HOLLY
segments.en: I guess that will help me when I apply for a real job.
segments.zh: 我想这对我申请真正的工作会有帮助。

segments.speaker: TUTOR
segments.en: Exactly – think ahead – remember what your ambitions are and keep them in mind.
segments.zh: 正是——要提前考虑——记住你的抱负是什么，并牢记在心。

segments.speaker: HOLLY
segments.en: Definitely.
segments.zh: 当然。

items.prompt: Holly has chosen the Orion Stadium placement because

pairs.note: 题干问原因，原文中'attracts me'直接表达了原因，是因果关系替换。
题干中的'chosen'（选择）与原文'attracts me'（吸引我）是同义替换，表示她选择的原因是被吸引。

paraphrase.explain: 通过定位词Orion Stadium找到原文，然后听到'attracts me'表示原因，对应题干问的because，从而选出答案。

items.prompt: Which aspect of safety does Dr Green emphasise most?

pairs.note: 题干问'最强调'，原文用'include'表示包含，属于同义替换。
题干中的'safety'被具体化为'keeping everyone within the boundary'，是解释性替换。

paraphrase.explain: 通过题干关键词'safety'和'emphasise most'，在原文中找到对应的'keeping everyone within the boundary'和'include'，从而定位到答案。

items.prompt: What does Dr Green say about the spectators?

pairs.note: spectators（观众）在原文中被具体化为proud parent（骄傲的家长），属于上下义替换。

pairs.p: trying to get a snap of their child and you want them to move elsewhere
pairs.note: 题干问Dr Green对观众的看法，原文描述家长拍照时想让他们移动，这是具体行为的描述。

paraphrase.explain: 通过定位词spectators找到原文中的proud parent，再根据题干问Dr Green的看法，原文中描述家长拍照时想让他们移动，对应选项A（他们有时会妨碍到别人）。

items.prompt: What has affected the schedule in the past?

pairs.note: 题干说影响日程，原文说导致严重延误，意思相同。
过去对应去年，是具体时间点。

paraphrase.explain: 定位句提到去年因有人受伤导致严重延误，与题干问过去影响日程的因素一致，因此选B。

pairs.note: 题干Communication（沟通）被具体化为原文中的'what you say'（你所说的内容），属于同义转述。

paraphrase.explain: 题干关键词'Communication'在定位句中被具体化为'what you say'，通过这一同义替换定位到原文。

pairs.note: 题干名词对应原文名词短语，表示同一概念

paraphrase.explain: 题干关键词'Organisation'在定位句中替换为'organisational skills'，直接对应，帮助定位答案所在句。

pairs.note: 题干说时间管理，原文用'截止日期临近'来具体化时间管理的情境。

paraphrase.explain: 题干中的'Time management'在原文中被具体化为'deadline approaches'，通过这种具体情境的替换来定位答案。

pairs.note: 题干关键词与原文完全一致，直接定位。

paraphrase.explain: 题干中的'Creativity'在原文首句出现，直接对应，因此答案B正确。

pairs.note: 领导力被解释为承担最终责任，这是对领导力核心的概括。

paraphrase.explain: 题干中的'Leadership'对应原文中的'ultimate responsibility'，通过同义替换定位到选项F。

pairs.note: Networking 被解释为求职过程中的活动，原文用'apply for a real job'表达相同含义。

paraphrase.explain: 题干关键词Networking在原文中没有直接出现，但通过'apply for a real job'这一求职相关表述间接对应，因此定位到该句。
